decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the game through utilizing powerful mana ramp with cards like Birds of Paradise, Ignoble Hierarch, and Gaea's Cradle. It also aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Collector Ouphe and Endurance, while maintaining a strong board presence with creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Wight of the Reliquary. The deck can quickly ramp up to play big threats like Craterhoof Behemoth or Grist, the Hunger Tide, and can search for key pieces with Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order. It is a versatile and resilient control deck with a strong mana base.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures and lands to power out Hogaak, while also utilizing cards like Elvish Reclaimer and Wight of the Reliquary to search for key lands like Bayou and Bojuka Bog. It also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt opponents' game plans. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and powerful combo finish using Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis, and relies on graveyard recursion and land synergies to achieve its win condition.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory, supported by efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben slows down the opponent's spells, while cards like Goblin Bombardment and Static Prison disrupt their strategy. The deck also includes land destruction with Wasteland and Karakas for added control. The goal is to apply constant pressure and maximize damage output to win the game swiftly.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize fast mana acceleration and powerful draw spells to assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game. The deck aims to generate a large amount of mana quickly with cards like Chrome Mox, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Dark Ritual, and then use cards like Burning Wish and Beseech the Mirror to tutor for the necessary combo pieces. The Fantasticar is a key card in this deck, providing additional mana acceleration and allowing for explosive plays. The deck can win through storming off with Tendrils of Agony or utilizing Song of Creation to draw into key cards for the win.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of artifacts and blue spells to quickly set up a board presence and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. By utilizing cards like Mishra's Bauble, Mox Opal, and Urza's Saga, the deck can generate a lot of mana and card advantage early in the game. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Thoughtcast provide additional card draw and recursion options, while cards like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void help control the board and protect your key pieces. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on quickly getting threats onto the battlefield and disrupting the opponent's hand with discard spells. It aims to control the board with removal spells and efficient creatures while utilizing graveyard interaction to bring back powerful threats. The deck also includes a land destruction package to disrupt opponent's mana base and control the tempo of the game. The aggressive nature of the deck allows it to apply pressure early and maintain a strong board presence throughout the game.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using counter spells and removal cards to disrupt the opponent's strategy while efficiently deploying threats like Stoneforge Mystic and equipment cards. The deck aims to maintain board control and tempo advantage through efficient card draw and manipulation with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder. The inclusion of powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler, also adds to the deck's controlling strategy by providing additional utility and disruption options. Overall, the deck is focused on controlling the game and outmaneuvering the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells like Force of Will and Stifle, along with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. It also utilizes card advantage engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler to outvalue the opponent. The deck aims to control the game by managing the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Karakas, while also presenting threats like Murktide Regent and Geist of Saint Traft to close out the game. The deck's main goal is to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

This combo deck focuses on utilizing Aluren to cast creatures for free and abuse their abilities to win the game quickly. It includes powerful creatures like Acererak, Atraxa, and Emrakul to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also contains essential spells like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Show and Tell to maintain control and set up the combo. With mana acceleration from Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, as well as card selection from Ponder and Stock Up, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize artifacts and creatures to quickly and aggressively control the board and overwhelm your opponent. By combining artifact synergies with powerful creatures like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Goblin Welder, you can generate card advantage and disrupt your opponent's strategy. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will for protection and disruption. The mana base is designed to support the artifact theme, with cards like Urza's Saga and various fetch lands to ensure consistent and efficient mana production.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. With a mix of creatures like Amped Raptor and Voice of Victory, backed up by removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. The inclusion of Jegantha, the Wellspring as a companion provides additional resources and flexibility. The deck also incorporates disruption tools like Static Prison and Wasteland to control the opponent's board state and mana base, allowing the aggro strategy to shine.

The key concept of this deck is to assemble the Cephalid Breakfast combo, using cards like Narcomoeba, Nomads en-Kor, and Cephalid Illusionist to enable powerful interactions that can lead to a win condition. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares to protect the combo, along with card selection and card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder. Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide additional utility and support for the deck's strategy, making it a versatile and potent combo deck in the Legacy format.

The deck focuses on using graveyard recursion and sacrifice effects to quickly fill the graveyard and bring back powerful creatures like Hogaak, all while disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize. The deck also runs land destruction to disrupt the opponent's mana base and protect its own powerful lands like Boseiju. The key goal of the deck is to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of speed, disruption, and powerful creatures.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Koma, World-Eater, and then bring them back into play using cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. The deck includes various ways to filter through the deck, protect the combo pieces with counter spells like Force of Will and Force of Negation, and disrupt the opponent's strategy with cards like Thoughtseize and Echoing Truth. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures.

This deck focuses on quickly and efficiently reanimating powerful creatures from the graveyard, such as Archon of Cruelty, Atraxa, Grand Unifier, and Griselbrand. Using cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana, along with spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring creatures back, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful threats early in the game. Additionally, cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize help disrupt the opponent's game plan while Collective Brutality provides additional utility. The deck's strategy revolves around executing its combo pieces to bring out powerful creatures and dominate the battlefield.

This deck revolves around assembling a powerful combo using cards like Voltaic Key, Skateboard, and Manifold Key to generate infinite mana and card draw with Mystic Forge. Chalice of the Void and Lotus Petal help control the board while Mox Opal and Grim Monolith provide additional mana ramp. The Fantasticar acts as a win condition, alongside The One Ring and Kozilek's Command. Glaring Fleshraker helps disrupt opponents' strategies. Urza's Saga helps tutor for key pieces, while Ancient Tomb, City of Traitors, and Petrified Hamlet provide additional mana sources.

The deck is focused on controlling the opponent through a combination of removal spells, counter spells, and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize. By having a mix of different lands, the deck is able to access a variety of colors in order to play a diverse range of spells. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Quantum Riddler provide card advantage and potential win conditions, while Leyline Binding can lock down key threats. The deck also features a toolbox aspect with Life from the Loam and Up the Beanstalk to fetch specific lands as needed.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful artifact creatures and spells to apply pressure to the opponent quickly. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Goblin Welder help to recur key artifacts from the graveyard, while Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer provide additional threats. The deck also includes efficient mana acceleration with Mox Opal and Lotus Petal, as well as disruptive elements like Force of Will. Urza's Saga helps to further establish a dominant board presence. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful artifact synergies.

The main strategy of this deck is to quickly assemble powerful artifact creatures and utilize them to overwhelm the opponent. It focuses on using cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Goblin Welder to constantly recur and reuse artifacts from the graveyard. The deck also includes a variety of artifact cards like Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal to generate resources and keep the pressure on the opponent. The presence of powerful artifact lands like Seat of the Synod and Urza's Saga further enhances the deck's ability to consistently produce threats and maintain board presence.

The primary strategy of this deck is to establish a strong lock on the opponent by utilizing artifacts to control the board and limit their options. Cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void restrict the opponent's ability to play spells, while Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storm provide additional control options. Eldrazi Confluence and Kozilek's Command offer versatile removal options, while Planar Nexus can disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to establish a powerful artifact-based prison to control the game and secure victory.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on applying early pressure with efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Ocelot Pride while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Swords to Plowshares. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional aggression and resilience, while Goblin Bombardment can act as a finisher. Static Prison helps control the board, and Wasteland can disrupt the opponent's mana base. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently while maintaining a disruptive game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through powerful spells and disruptive effects while ramping up mana with Cloudpost / Tron lands. The deck utilizes powerful cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Karn, the Great Creator to dominate the board and shut down opposing strategies. Force of Will and Force of Negation provide protection while Eldrazi Confluence and Kozilek's Command offer versatile removal options. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with massive threats and spells while maintaining control of the game with a solid mana base.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board through efficient creature removal and disruption spells, while also utilizing powerful ramp spells to accelerate into big threats like Craterhoof Behemoth. The deck aims to utilize the synergy between cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Gaea's Cradle to generate large amounts of mana and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and creatures. The inclusion of cards like Thoughtseize and Collector Ouphe also allows the deck to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own key pieces.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and graveyard recursion to generate infinite loops and combos, often involving sacrificing and recurring artifacts to create powerful synergies. Key cards like Goblin Welder, Emry, Lurker of the Loch, and Urza's Saga enable the deck to set up chains of actions that lead to explosive plays and overwhelming board presence. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently, leveraging its artifact-centric strategy to overwhelm opponents and secure victory through powerful synergistic interactions.

The main focus of this deck is to slow down the opponent with various taxing effects while assembling a powerful land engine. By using cards like Sphere of Resistance and Malevolent Rumble, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage with Life from the Loam and Exploration. The key win condition is to assemble the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token while controlling the board with cards like Ghost Quarter and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. The deck uses lands as its primary resource to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent.

The deck focuses on using lands and control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful combos with cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. It aims to generate value through card advantage with Life from the Loam and Exploration, while also utilizing disruptive cards like Wasteland and Mox Diamond to slow down the opponent's progress. The deck's main win condition revolves around creating a 20/20 indestructible Marit Lage token by combining Dark Depths with Thespian's Stage, making it difficult for the opponent to deal with such a threat.

This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggressive creatures like Wight of the Reliquary, Scythecat Cub, and Badgermole Cub, supported by mana acceleration from cards like Ignoble Hierarch and Noble Hierarch. The deck also focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Wasteland, while also having access to powerful utility lands like Gaea's Cradle and Boseiju, Who Endures. The deck's versatility is further enhanced by the toolbox nature of Green Sun's Zenith, allowing for different creatures to be searched for based on the situation.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures and then reanimating them for a game-winning combo. It includes cards like Careful Study and Thoughtseize to quickly discard creatures, while also utilizing spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back onto the battlefield. With counterspells like Force of Will and Daze for protection, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with powerful threats like Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. This combo-reanimator strategy allows for explosive plays and fast victories in Legacy 1 vs 1 matchups.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly bring out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier by using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing cards such as Omniscience and Stock Up, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early in the game. In addition, cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Force of Will provide card selection and protection to help achieve the combo win condition. The deck also includes mana acceleration with Lotus Petal and fast lands like Ancient Tomb to further expedite its strategy.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Doomsday combo using cards like Lotus Petal, Dark Ritual, and The Fantasticar to generate mana and dig through the deck with Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thoughtseize. Thassa's Oracle is used as the win condition once the combo is assembled, with Lion's Eye Diamond providing additional mana acceleration. The inclusion of Force of Will and Daze provides protection for the combo pieces, while Cavern of Souls ensures key spells can't be countered. The deck is designed to be fast and efficient, allowing for quick wins in the Legacy format.

The main focus of this deck is to control the tempo of the game through efficient use of card draw, counterspells, and removal spells, while also applying pressure with aggressive creatures. By utilizing cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent, along with disruptive spells like Daze and Force of Will, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and close out the game quickly. The mana base is optimized for consistency and maximizing the effectiveness of cards like Wasteland. Overall, the deck seeks to outpace and outmaneuver opponents through a combination of aggression and control.

The focus of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt opponent's strategy with counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage while setting up card advantage engines like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck aims to control the board with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of the Skies, while also deploying threats like Murktide Regent. The mana base is designed to support the control elements of the deck with access to different colors and utility lands like Wasteland. The deck aims to outvalue opponents through efficient spells and card advantage engines.
